ops.title: Cross-repo release + orchestration traps from the per-occurrence guard-rewrite rollout (cc-transcript v13.2.0 → capt-hook v9.15.0 → ccx pack 0.6.0)
ops.body:
Traps hit while shipping the per-occurrence rewrite train (2026-07-14/16); the design record lives in cc-notes (doc "Size-gated cat guard and per-occurrence rewrites (pack 0.6.0)" + umbrella task ade3276).

- **`verify-tag-on-main` lives in the REUSABLE workflow half** (yasyf/homebrew-tap release-pypi-build), not the caller's release-pypi.yml — checking only the caller yields a false "no branch guard". The gate is `git merge-base --is-ancestor $GITHUB_SHA origin/main`, so a patch-branch release can pass it by **merging the release branch into main**: the tag's own history stays clean (v13.1.0 + cherry-pick only) while the tagged commit becomes a main ancestor. That save shipped cc-transcript v13.2.0 without releasing the in-flight v14 inversion.
- **`gh run list --commit <sha>` returns empty for abbreviated SHAs** — silently, exit 0. Two dud CI watches before spotting it. Always pass the full 40-char sha.
- **Concurrent-session jj rebases invalidate your rebase assumptions**: my `jj rebase -r X -d main` reported "already in place" (another session had rebased the shared stack) and reparented `@` OFF my commit — working-tree files snapped back to pre-change content and looked reverted. The commit held all the work. Verify the commit (`jj diff --from main --to X`), not the working tree, before panicking; test it in a `jj workspace add` throwaway.
- **Usage-limit/API-stall workflow failures leave real work in the tree** — a "failed" fan-out had burned 263k tokens writing near-complete migrations (ls, vcs). Audit `git diff --stat` + run the tests before re-dispatching; brief re-run agents to "inspect and finish, don't rewrite".
- **capt-hook is v14-blocked**: cc-transcript's Rust views inversion drops PyO3 `subclass` on `ToolUseBlock`, and capt-hook's `LenientToolUseBlock(ToolUseBlock)` dies at import. Any future cc-transcript floor bump past `<14` needs that resolved first (cc-notes backlog bug 25941c1).
- **Version races on shared repos**: the concurrent session took capt-hook v9.14.0 + staged the 9.15.0 CHANGELOG section mid-train — re-check `git tag` + PyPI immediately before tagging, and append to a staged unreleased CHANGELOG section rather than adding a duplicate.
- `Rewrite(pattern=...)` in capt-hook inline tests is a **substring**, not a regex — `.*` patterns fail silently-confusingly (cost two agents time; now called out in migration briefs).
